Closes G022 Check 6 'required specialist phase missing: stabilize' finding for spec 031 (1 of the remaining 2 missing-phase findings raised by the 2026-05-23 strict reconcile pass; security follows per BUG-031-006 Scope 5 sequence). Outcome: n/a with provenance — zero stability/flakiness/resource risk in the BUG-031-006 change manifest. Bounded run (≈4 min wall, within the ≤5-min subagent budget). Review surface: (1) tests/stress/ml_readiness_timeout_stress_test.go (new 280 LOC, 3 test funcs); (2) scripts/runtime/go-stress.sh (+30 LOC --run selector); (3) scripts/runtime/go-integration.sh (additive --go-run plumbing); (4) tests/stress/readiness/canary_test.go (additive). Domain-by-domain audit (6 domains, all clean): (a) Performance — wall time dominated by intentional 2s SLA-boundary waits (design.md §92 SMACKEREL_ML_READINESS_TIMEOUT_OVERRIDE compress hook); 4.574s total for all 3 funcs captured by bubbles.test (exit 0); no N+1, no caching defect, no avoidable allocation. (b) Infrastructure/Deployment — zero Docker/Compose/container-lifecycle changes; new test is hermetic in-process httptest.NewServer (no live stack dependency, no port contention since httptest binds to OS-allocated ephemeral port, auto-released by defer mockML.Close()). (c) Configuration — sstReadinessTimeout reads SMACKEREL_ML_READINESS_TIMEOUT alias → ML_READINESS_TIMEOUT_S canonical → t.Fatalf when both empty; zero hidden defaults; satisfies smackerel-no-defaults policy; adversarial case 4 fails loud. (d) Build/CI — //go:build stress tag isolates the test from unit/integration builds; runner adds explicit per-package skip-when-no-match guard so --go-run selectors that match zero tests in a sibling stress sub-package (agent, drive, readiness) no longer surface as failure. (e) Reliability — timing tolerance [boundary−500ms, boundary+2s] (≈4× CI slack) on SLA path; compressed variant 2s ± [−500ms, +1500ms] (≈4× slack); context.WithTimeout + defer cancel prevents goroutine leaks; sync/atomic.Int32 for probe counter prevents data race; defer mockML.Close() ensures clean server shutdown; requireDisposableStack(t) fail-fasts on dev/prod markers at top of every test func. (f) Resource Usage — zero persistent state, zero file I/O, zero DB/NATS connections, zero goroutine pool growth; wall time bounded by boundary+5s safety margin; no log volume amplification. Runner change is procedural bash with no parallelism / shared state / resource impact (no xargs -P, no background jobs). Production source modified by this BUG: zero (already verified by bubbles.regression). No edits applied to the BUG change surface. Verdict: 🟢 N/A WITH PROVENANCE. No findings to route. Adds 'stabilize' to execution.completedPhaseClaims per Scope 5 G022 closure. Next required owner per Scope 5 sequence: bubbles.security.

(c) PII / env-specific values — zero real hostnames/IPs/usernames/tailnet IDs/RFC 6598 CGNAT addresses; stack markers in requireDisposableStack are SST-governed port numbers documented in .github/copilot-instructions.md; httptest.NewServer binds 127.0.0.1 ephemeral (generic loopback). (d) OWASP Top 10 — A01/A02/A08 N/A; A03 (injection) — Go %q quoting + bash array expansion + go test -run regex consumed by Go framework not shell; A05 (misconfig) — POSITIVE control: requireDisposableStack refuses to execute against dev/prod stack; A06 (vulnerable deps) — zero new dependencies, stdlib only (net/http, net/http/httptest, sync/atomic, context, time, strconv, strings, os, testing); A09 (logging) — failure messages use %q for SST markers, no credential exposure; A10 (SSRF) — httptest URL is internal ephemeral, passed only to WaitForMLReady which probes the mock. (e) Dependency vulnerability scan — N/A no new external dependencies. (f) Trust boundary — test crosses no trust boundary; in-process only; no network egress; no DB/NATS/Ollama connection; no file I/O. Runner-change security profile: case-statement arg parser with empty-guard, array-expanded "${go_test_args[@]}" at invocation, no eval/backticks/$(...) of user input. Cross-product check: BUG fully internal to spec 031 governance; QF Companion Boundary (Principle 10) N/A. Production source modified by this BUG: zero (already verified by bubbles.regression). No edits applied to the BUG change surface. Verdict: 🔒 SECURE / 🟢 N/A WITH PROVENANCE. No findings to route. Adds 'security' to execution.completedPhaseClaims per Scope 5 G022 closure.
